$schema_note: Per-box engine configuration TEMPLATE. install.sh / provision-anthology-client.sh resolve every <CLIENT_*> and <LABEL:*> slot per box as the node user. Credentials appear by LABEL only, never by value. The client-facing platform name is Convert and Flow in every surface. Nothing Anthropic ships in this file.

engine.model_map_ref: config/model-map.template.json (resolved to model-map.json per box by preflight.sh)

intake.forms_base_note: The PUBLIC base URL of the client's Convert and Flow author-intake form, resolved per box by install.sh / provision-anthology-client.sh (NULL in this template; never committed, never hardcoded). scripts/anthology_book.py appends /anthology-intake?anthology_id=<minted>&book_name=<url-encoded> to build each book's shareable intake link (the Book ID rides the URL param onto the form's hidden anthology_id field). When unset, the minter emits a RELATIVE link and flags forms_base_configured=false; the operator sets the base per box or passes --forms-base.
intake.tenant_check: payload location must equal the anthology registry Convert and Flow Location binding; a mismatch lands in Exceptions (tenant_mismatch), never a guess
intake.keying: contact_id plus anthology_id composite; never email; unroutable submissions land in the exceptions queue with the raw payload and a typed reason

delivery.drive_root_note: The operator's EXISTING anyone-can-read delivery root (PRD 3.7). drive-tree-provision.py verifies reachability at preflight and NEVER creates a new root. Tree below it: Producer, then Anthology, then Participant. Per-document sharing is anyone-with-link VIEW only.

registry_defaults.pipeline_binding_note: Onboarding AUTO-PROVISIONS the standard Anthology pipeline in the CLIENT's own Convert and Flow account through the CLIENT's OWN private integration token; binding to a pre-existing pipeline is an explicit override, never the default.
registry_defaults.caf_stage_map: per-anthology; drives the per-gate Convert and Flow pipeline-stage update; NEVER hardcoded

nudge_policy.all_other_repeats: manual via the board re-send button, rate-limited
nudge_policy.recipient_rule: ALWAYS resolved from the ledger row for the given contact_id (participant gates) or the producer record (producer gates); NO literal recipient exists anywhere in the engine
nudge_policy.silence_doctrine: The engine sends NOTHING else to any client, ever. Operator surfaces are verbose; client surfaces are silent.

hold_queue_policy.credit_out: durable HOLD with full state; ONE deduped founder Telegram alert through the OpenClaw gateway; the daily tick retries the chain and resumes from the exact cursor
hold_queue_policy.callback_lost: bounded re-poll, then hold plus alert
hold_queue_policy.gate_wait: NO timeout exists; the ledger holds the cursor for weeks or months at zero cost; 7-day stuck highlighting plus ONE deduped auto re-nudge
hold_queue_policy.strike_out: 3 internal QC attempts per deliverable, then HOLD plus a single deduped founder alert carrying the failing checks and the best draft; standards never relaxed

gates.participant_token_scope: single-gate-scoped, HMAC over participant_key + gate id + expiry; expires on gate closure; foreign, expired, and replayed tokens are refused
gates.both_door_rule: the emailed nudge deep link (participant) and the board card view (producer) hit the SAME endpoint and the SAME anthology_state.py subcommand; logic lives in ONE place

board.auth: HMAC (x-webhook-signature over WEBHOOK_SECRET) plus Bearer (MC_API_TOKEN); Skill 32's existing pair, reused never duplicated
board.fail_soft: board unreachability NEVER blocks the pipeline; the ledger remains the truth and the card reconciles on the daily tick
board.status_mapping: card status mirrors Participants.stage_cursor; producer-facing deliverables land the card in the review column (the chapter-approval queue); ONLY the independent QC scorer at or above 8.5 promotes review to done; the engine never self-promotes
board.department_seed: the Anthology department is seeded via Skill 32 add-department.sh (POST /api/departments create:true) during provisioning, idempotent and verified by reading the department back

cron.daily_tick: one cron entry: smoke test (balance endpoints only, at or under one cent), hold-queue aging, mirror reconcile, stuck-gate re-nudge policy
cron.no_heartbeat: NO heartbeat cron ever; guard-cron-inventory.py proves exactly the one daily tick and that churn leaves zero recurring jobs

credential_labels.note: Resolved SET or NOT SET only, never by value, across all three client env stores (live process env first). See PRD Section 14.
